articles.title: Stock Market Today (June 30, 2026): Tech recovery continues with Nasdaq, S&P 500 jumping
articles.summary: Stocks edged higher Tuesday on tech recovery. Nasdaq +1.28% midday, S&P 500 +0.03%. AVAV +20% on earnings beat. CNXC -19% on miss. MSTR -7.2% on BTC sale plan. U.S.-Iran agreed to halt hostilities. Supreme Court protected Fed independence 5-4. Apple supply chain breached.
articles.lead_paragraph: Stocks edged higher Tuesday. Markets rallied sharply on Monday, snapping a five-day losing streak as technology shares rebounded.
articles.extracted_text: Dow closed at record high Monday; Tuesday +0.14%. Nasdaq +1.28% midday. Russell 2000 flat. QQQ +1.1% to $731.91. SMH +0.82% premarket. Key movers: AVAV +20% (earnings beat), CNXC -19% (earnings miss), MSTR -7.2% (BTC sale). U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz reopening. Supreme Court 5-4 ruling protects Fed independence. Apple supply chain breach via Tata Electronics. Nike (NKE) after-hours earnings preview. Elon Musk net worth back above $1T.

articles.title: Stock market today: Live updates (June 29-30, 2026) - CNBC
articles.summary: Key U.S. indexes rose Tuesday led by semiconductors. SMH +3%. Consumer confidence 91.2 vs 94.2 est. JOLTS 7.59M vs 7.3M est. Gold worst quarter since 2013, WTI worst since Q1 2020. AVAV +30% on beat. Iran-US talks in Doha. BoE held at 3.75%. ECB Lagarde defends rate hikes.
articles.lead_paragraph: Key U.S. indexes rose on Tuesday, the final day of the first half and second quarter, led by semiconductor stocks. Dow +0.3%, S&P 500 +0.7%, Nasdaq +1.3% at 2:51 PM EDT.
articles.extracted_text: Q2 gains: S&P 500 ~14%, Nasdaq ~20% (best since Q2 2020), Dow >12% (best since Q4 2022). Russell 2000 >21% YTD (best since 1991). SMH ~+70% in Q2. Consumer confidence 91.2 (below 94.2 consensus). JOLTS 7.59M (above 7.3M forecast). Gold -13% Q2, worst since Q2 2013. WTI -30% Q2, worst since Q1 2020. Iran-US talks in Doha. ECB Lagarde: no more unconventional tools needed. BoE held 3.75% (7-2). Fed market pricing higher rate odds. Earnings: AVAV beat (EPS $1.84 vs $1.46, revenue $642M vs $559M). CNXC miss (EPS $2.63 vs $2.64).

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Advances, on Track to Wrap Quarter With 20% Gain - WSJ
articles.summary: Mid-afternoon snapshot: DJIA 52,325 (+0.27%), S&P 500 7,501 (+0.82%), Nasdaq 26,191 (+1.43%), Russell 2000 3,028 (+0.58%). 10Y 4.422%. VIX 16.54 (-6.29%). Gold $4,046. WTI $69.95. DXY 97.53. Bitcoin $58,583. Iran-US talks in Qatar. Yen near 40-year low.
articles.lead_paragraph: U.S. stocks are closing out a blockbuster second quarter with fresh momentum. S&P 500 up 14% through Monday, Nasdaq up 20%.
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500 +14% Q2, Nasdaq +20% Q2 - biggest quarterly gains since Q2 2020. Despite significant volatility including last week's tech sell-off. Drivers: Iran-U.S. talks in Qatar, yen near 40-year low, oil gave up gains. Gold/silver pressured by stronger dollar and rate expectations. KBW Bank Index -0.49%. U.S. airline stocks soaring (+20% in June on cheaper jet fuel). Warsh wants Fed to talk less.

articles.title: Nike Q4 2026 Earnings Report Summary - MarketBeat
articles.summary: Nike Q4 FY2026: EPS $0.72 vs $0.13 consensus (454% beat). Revenue $10.97B vs $10.85B est. Stock closed -0.96% at $41.08, then fell -4.51% after-hours to $39.23. China weakness and slow turnaround overshadow beat. Conference call at 5PM ET.
articles.lead_paragraph: Nike reported Q4 FY2026 EPS of $0.72 crushing the $0.13 consensus estimate by 454%. Revenue $10.97B beat $10.85B estimate.
articles.extracted_text: NKE Q4 FY2026: EPS $0.72 vs $0.13 est (+454% surprise). Revenue $10.97B vs $10.85B est (+$122M beat). Regular close $41.08 (-0.96%). Extended trading $39.23 (-4.51%). Despite blowout beat, stock sold off on ongoing China weakness, declining sales, and slow turnaround. Conference call 5PM ET. Press release 8-K filed.

articles.title: Nike shares pop on earnings and revenue beat - CNBC Video
articles.summary: CNBC Closing Bell Overtime: Nike shares initially popped on earnings and revenue beat after the close, though the regular session saw -2.53% decline.
articles.lead_paragraph: Nike reported earnings that beat expectations, along with revenue outperformance. Shares moved higher following the release.
articles.extracted_text: NKE earnings beat on EPS and revenue. Initial after-hours pop. Segment from Closing Bell: Overtime with Brandon Gomez. Regular session decline -2.53% preceding release.

articles.title: Constellation Brands Q1 FY2027 Earnings - Digrin Calendar
articles.summary: After-close earnings: STZ (Constellation Brands) Q1 FY2027 EPS $3.43 vs $3.27 est, revenue $2.4B in-line. PRGS (Progress Software) EPS $1.62 vs $1.52 est, revenue $253.5M vs $247.6M est.
articles.lead_paragraph: Earnings calendar June 30: STZ reported EPS $3.43 beating $3.27 estimate. PRGS reported EPS $1.62 beating $1.52 estimate.
articles.extracted_text: STZ: EPS $3.43 vs $3.27 est, revenue $2.4B vs $2.4B est. PRGS: EPS $1.62 vs $1.52 est, revenue $253.5M vs $247.6M est. After-market close reports. NKE results pending at time of scrape.

articles.title: AeroVironment Q4 2026 earnings beat, AVAV stock soars - Quartz
articles.summary: AeroVironment Q4 FY2026: adjusted EPS $1.84 vs $1.46 est, revenue $641.6M vs $556M est. Backlog grew to $1.2B. Stock +30% on the day.
articles.lead_paragraph: The drone maker posted Q4 adjusted EPS of $1.84 versus an estimate of $1.46, with revenue of $641.6 million against an estimate of $556 million. Stock surged 30%.
articles.extracted_text: AVAV Q4 FY2026: Adjusted EPS $1.84 vs $1.46 est. Revenue $641.6M vs $556M est. Backlog $1.2B. Net income $63.17M vs $16.66M prior year. Stock +30% on session. Driven by defense spending and drone demand.

articles.title: Strategy's 'HODL' Playbook Is Under Pressure - Barron's
articles.summary: Strategy (MSTR) authorized up to $1.25B in Bitcoin sales to fund $2.55B reserve for dividends/interest. Also $1B buyback. STRC dividend +11.5% to 12%. MSTR -6.2%. Marks end of 'HODL' era. BTC holdings 847,363 at avg $75,651.
articles.lead_paragraph: Strategy is formally replacing the 'never sell' mantra with a capital-management framework that treats Bitcoin as a flexible treasury asset.
articles.extracted_text: MSTR: Up to $1.25B BTC sales authorized. $2.55B USD reserve for dividends/interest. $1B common stock buyback. STRC dividend 11.5% → 12%. BTC holdings: 847,363 at $75,651 avg (BTC now below $60K). mNAV ~1.04. CEO Phong Le: 'evolving from one-way capital issuance to active capital management.' MSTR -6.2%, STRC +2%.

articles.title: US 2Y/10Y/30Y Treasury Yields June 30, 2026 - Trading Economics
articles.summary: Treasury yields: 2Y 4.14% (+2 bp), 10Y 4.42% (+4 bp), 30Y 4.89% (+2 bp) on June 30, 2026. Yields ticking higher across the curve.
articles.lead_paragraph: U.S. Treasury yields rose on June 30. 2Y at 4.14%, 10Y at 4.42%, 30Y at 4.89%.
articles.extracted_text: 2Y: 4.14% (+2 bp day). 10Y: 4.42% (+4 bp). 30Y: 4.89% (+2 bp). 10Y range: 4.361-4.408%. Prev close 4.374%. Yields rising on hawkish Fed expectations and quarter-end. Gold pressured by higher yields.

articles.title: Apple Supply Chain Breach: iPhone 18 Pro data leaked via Tata Electronics - TheStreet
articles.summary: Sensitive Apple component/supplier lists and iPhone 18 Pro photos posted on dark web after ransomware group breached Tata Electronics. Could strain Apple-Tata relationship. iPhone 18 launch still expected September 2026.
articles.lead_paragraph: Sensitive lists of components and suppliers, along with photos of Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models, were posted on the dark web after a ransomware group stole data from supplier Tata Electronics.
articles.extracted_text: Apple supply chain breach via Tata Electronics. Component lists, supplier data, iPhone 18 Pro photos leaked on dark web. Ransomware group responsible. May strain Apple-Tata partnership (key non-China supplier). iPhone 18 Pro/Pro Max launch still expected Sep 2026. Apple also faces memory/storage chip price cost pressures.
